lines.gurmukhi: ਤਨ ਮਹਿ ਕਾਮੁ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੁ ਹਉ ਮਮਤਾ ਕਠਿਨ ਪੀਰ ਅਤਿ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: tan meh kaam krodh hau mamataa katthin peer at bhaaree |
lines.english: Within the body are sexual desire, anger, egotism and attachment. This pain is so great, and so difficult to endure.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਚ ਸਬਦ ਬਿਨੁ ਕਬਹੁ ਨ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਜਨਮੁ ਭਇਓ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: saach sabad bin kabahu na chhoottas birathaa janam bheo |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Without the True Word of the Shabad, you shall never be released, and your life shall be totally useless. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਾਮ ਜਪਹੁ ਰਸੁ ਰਸਨਾ ਇਨ ਬਿਧਿ ਤਰੁ ਤੂ ਤਾਰੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: guramukh raam japahu ras rasanaa in bidh tar too taaree |2|
lines.english: As Gurmukh, chant the Lord's Name, and savor it with your tongue; in this way, you shall cross over to the other side. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਹਰੇ ਕਰਨ ਅਕਲਿ ਭਈ ਹੋਛੀ ਸਬਦ ਸਹਜੁ ਨਹੀ ਬੂਝਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: bahare karan akal bhee hochhee sabad sehaj nahee boojhiaa |
lines.english: Your ears are deaf, and your intellect is worthless, and still, you do not intuitively understand the Word of the Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨਮੁ ਪਦਾਰਥੁ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਹਾਰਿਆ ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਅੰਧੁ ਨ ਸੂਝਿਆ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: janam padaarath manamukh haariaa bin gur andh na soojhiaa |3|
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh wastes this priceless human life and loses it. Without the Guru, the blind person cannot see. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਣਵਤਿ ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ॥੪॥੨॥੩॥
lines.translit: pranavat naanak guramukh chhoottas raam naam liv laagee |4|2|3|
lines.english: - prays Nanak, as Gurmukh, he is released. He is lovingly attuned to the Naam, the Name of the Lord. ||4||||2||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਹੈ ਉਦਾਸੁ ਆਸ ਨਿਰਾਸਾ ਸਹਜ ਧਿਆਨਿ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: rahai udaas aas niraasaa sehaj dhiaan bairaagee |
lines.english: Whoever remains detached and free of desire in the midst of desire - and whoever, unattached, intuitively meditates on the Celestial Lord

lines.gurmukhi: ਨੇਤ੍ਰੀ ਧੁੰਧਿ ਕਰਨ ਭਏ ਬਹਰੇ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਜਾਨੀ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: netree dhundh karan bhe bahare manamukh naam na jaanee |1|
lines.english: His eyes are dim, his ears are deaf, and yet, the self-willed manmukh does not know the Naam. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੂੰਡੀ ਚਾਲ ਚਰਣ ਕਰ ਖਿਸਰੇ ਤੁਚਾ ਦੇਹ ਕੁਮਲਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: bhoonddee chaal charan kar khisare tuchaa deh kumalaanee |
lines.english: His walk becomes weak and clumsy, his feet and hands shake, and the skin of his body is withered and wrinkled.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Bhairao, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੁਲੇ ਕਿਆ ਪਾਇਆ ਜਗਿ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: andhule kiaa paaeaa jag aae |
lines.english: O blind man, what have you obtained by coming into the world?

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮੁ ਰਿਦੈ ਨਹੀ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਚਾਲੇ ਮੂਲੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: raam ridai nahee gur kee sevaa chaale mool gavaae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Lord is not in your heart, and you do not serve the Guru. After wasting your capital, you shall have to depart. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਹਵਾ ਰੰਗਿ ਨਹੀ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਤੀ ਜਬ ਬੋਲੈ ਤਬ ਫੀਕੇ ॥
lines.translit: jihavaa rang nahee har raatee jab bolai tab feeke |
lines.english: Your tongue is not imbued with the Love of the Lord; whatever you say is tasteless and insipid.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਤ ਜਨਾ ਕੀ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਵਿਆਪਸਿ ਪਸੂ ਭਏ ਕਦੇ ਹੋਹਿ ਨ ਨੀਕੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sant janaa kee nindaa viaapas pasoo bhe kade hohi na neeke |2|
lines.english: You indulge in slander of the Saints; becoming a beast, you shall never be noble. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕਾ ਰਸੁ ਵਿਰਲੀ ਪਾਇਆ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਏ ॥
lines.translit: amrit kaa ras viralee paaeaa satigur mel milaae |
lines.english: Only a few obtain the sublime essence of the Ambrosial Amrit, united in Union with the True Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਲਗੁ ਸਬਦ ਭੇਦੁ ਨਹੀ ਆਇਆ ਤਬ ਲਗੁ ਕਾਲੁ ਸੰਤਾਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jab lag sabad bhed nahee aaeaa tab lag kaal santaae |3|
lines.english: As long as the mortal does not come to understand the mystery of the Shabad, the Word of God, he shall continue to be tormented by death. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨ ਕੋ ਦਰੁ ਘਰੁ ਕਬਹੂ ਨ ਜਾਨਸਿ ਏਕੋ ਦਰੁ ਸਚਿਆਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: an ko dar ghar kabahoo na jaanas eko dar sachiaaraa |
lines.english: Whoever finds the door of the One True Lord, does not know any other house or door.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਪਰਮ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਕਹੈ ਵਿਚਾਰਾ ॥੪॥੩॥੪॥
lines.translit: gur parasaad param pad paaeaa naanak kahai vichaaraa |4|3|4|
lines.english: By Guru's Grace, I have obtained the supreme status; so says poor Nanak. ||4||3||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Bhairao, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਿਨੁ ਪਲੁ ਘੜੀ ਨਹੀ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਜਾਨਿਆ ਜਿਨਿ ਇਹੁ ਜਗਤੁ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: khin pal gharree nahee prabh jaaniaa jin ihu jagat upaaeaa |1|
lines.english: He does not know God, who created this world, for a moment, for even an instant. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਗਲੀ ਰੈਣਿ ਸੋਵਤ ਗਲਿ ਫਾਹੀ ਦਿਨਸੁ ਜੰਜਾਲਿ ਗਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sagalee rain sovat gal faahee dinas janjaal gavaaeaa |
lines.english: He spends the entire night in sleep; the noose is tied around his neck. His day is wasted in worldly entanglements.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨ ਰੇ ਕਿਉ ਛੂਟਸਿ ਦੁਖੁ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: man re kiau chhoottas dukh bhaaree |
lines.english: O mortal, how will you escape this terrible disaster?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਲੇ ਆਵਸਿ ਕਿਆ ਲੇ ਜਾਵਸਿ ਰਾਮ ਜਪਹੁ ਗੁਣਕਾਰੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa le aavas kiaa le jaavas raam japahu gunakaaree |1| rahaau |
lines.english: What did you bring with you, and what will you take away? Meditate on the Lord, the Most Worthy and Generous Lord. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਊਂਧਉ ਕਵਲੁ ਮਨਮੁਖ ਮਤਿ ਹੋਛੀ ਮਨਿ ਅੰਧੈ ਸਿਰਿ ਧੰਧਾ ॥
lines.translit: aoondhau kaval manamukh mat hochhee man andhai sir dhandhaa |
lines.english: The heart-lotus of the self-willed manmukh is upside-down; his intellect is shallow; his mind is blind, and his head is entangled in worldly affairs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਡਗਰੀ ਚਾਲ ਨੇਤ੍ਰ ਫੁਨਿ ਅੰਧੁਲੇ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਨਹੀ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: ddagaree chaal netr fun andhule sabad surat nahee bhaaee |
lines.english: Your steps are unsteady, and your eyes are blind; you are not aware of the Word of the Shabad, O Sibling of Destiny.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਲੁ ਬਿਕਾਲੁ ਸਦਾ ਸਿਰਿ ਤੇਰੈ ਬਿਨੁ ਨਾਵੈ ਗਲਿ ਫੰਧਾ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: kaal bikaal sadaa sir terai bin naavai gal fandhaa |2|
lines.english: Death and re-birth constantly hang over your head; without the Name, your neck shall be caught in the noose. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖੋਇਓ ਮੂਲੁ ਲਾਭੁ ਕਹ ਪਾਵਸਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਗਿਆਨ ਵਿਹੂਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: khoeo mool laabh keh paavas duramat giaan vihoone |
lines.english: He loses his capital - how can he earn any profit? The evil-minded person has no spiritual wisdom at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਬੇਦ ਤ੍ਰੈ ਗੁਣ ਹੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਅੰਧੁਲਉ ਧੰਧੁ ਕਮਾਈ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: saasatr bed trai gun hai maaeaa andhulau dhandh kamaaee |3|
lines.english: The Shaastras and the Vedas keep the mortal bound to the three modes of Maya, and so he performs his deeds blindly. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਬਦੁ ਬੀਚਾਰਿ ਰਾਮ ਰਸੁ ਚਾਖਿਆ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾਚਿ ਪਤੀਣੇ ॥੪॥੪॥੫॥
lines.translit: sabad beechaar raam ras chaakhiaa naanak saach pateene |4|4|5|
lines.english: Contemplating the Shabad, he drinks in the sublime essence of the Lord; O Nanak, his faith is confirmed in the Truth. ||4||4||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੈਰਉ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: bhairau mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Bhairao, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਰਹੈ ਦਿਨੁ ਰਾਤੀ ਰਾਮੁ ਰਸਨਿ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਾਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: gur kai sang rahai din raatee raam rasan rang raataa |
lines.english: He remains with the Guru, day and night, and his tongue savors the savory taste of the Lord's Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਵਰੁ ਨ ਜਾਣਸਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਪਛਾਣਸਿ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਜਾਣਿ ਪਛਾਤਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: avar na jaanas sabad pachhaanas antar jaan pachhaataa |1|
lines.english: He does not know any other; he realizes the Word of the Shabad. He knows and realizes the Lord deep within his own being. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਜਨੁ ਐਸਾ ਮੈ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: so jan aisaa mai man bhaavai |
lines.english: Such a humble person is pleasing to my mind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੁ ਮਾਰਿ ਅਪਰੰਪਰਿ ਰਾਤਾ ਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਵੈ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aap maar aparanpar raataa gur kee kaar kamaavai |1| rahaau |
lines.english: He conquers his self-conceit, and is imbued with the Infinite Lord. He serves the Guru. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਰੰਜਨੁ ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਆਦੇਸੋ ॥
lines.translit: antar baahar purakh niranjan aad purakh aadeso |
lines.english: Deep within my being, and outside as well, is the Immaculate Lord God. I bow humbly before that Primal Lord God.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਟ ਘਟ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਰਵਿ ਰਹਿਆ ਸਚੁ ਵੇਸੋ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: ghatt ghatt antar sarab nirantar rav rahiaa sach veso |2|
lines.english: Deep within each and every heart, and amidst all, the Embodiment of Truth is permeating and pervading. ||2||
